item.title: Volatile Chipmaker Stocks Emerge as Key Driver of S&P 500 Rally
item.description: Shares of chipmakers are increasingly dominating the S&P 500 Index, powering stocks to record highs while stirring concerns over the rally’s durability.

item.title: Emerging Carry Trade Rebounds With Real, Rand Among Favorites
item.description: The emerging-market carry trade has bounced back from its Iran war losses as surging crude oil prices reinforce expectations that interest rates will stay elevated and bolster the currencies of commodity exporters.

item.title: Malaysia’s Coalition Tensions Deepen as Anwar Floats Snap Poll
item.description: Fresh strains are emerging in Malaysia’s ruling alliance as political jockeying and oneupmanship among the coalition’s parties led Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim to raise the prospect of a snap general election if divisions deepen.

item.title: Israel’s Economy Slumps More Than Expected in the First Quarter
item.description: Israel’s economy slumped in the first quarter of the year when the fallout of the war with Iran imposed security-related shutdowns on businesses for more than a month.

item.title: Vietnam’s Largest Refinery Sees ‘Stable’ Operations Through June
item.description: Vietnam’s largest oil refinery continues to diversify crude supply and is confident that it can maintain stable operations through June, according to a company statement.

item.title: World Imbalances Trouble G-7 in Wake of Trump’s China Encounter
item.description: The lopsided foundations of world growth will focus global finance ministers this week in the aftermath of the summit that attempted to reset trade ties between its two biggest economies.

item.title: Samsung, Labor Union to Meet in ‘Last Chance’ to Avert Strike
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. and its largest labor union are set to resume negotiations Monday to avert a strike that the nation’s prime minister warned could wreak havoc on the economy.

item.title: Taxes and Trump Leave UK in a Worse State Than Starmer Found It
item.description: Weeks after his general election triumph, Keir Starmer sent voters a sobering message: “Things will get worse before we get better.”

item.title: Record Drone Attack on Moscow Kills Three, Targets Refinery
item.description: Russia’s capital and the surrounding Moscow region came under record drone attacks overnight, leaving at least three people killed and over a dozen wounded.

item.title: Australia Says Tax Changes Aim to Fix Broken Housing Market
item.description: Australia’s Treasurer Jim Chalmers on Sunday reiterated that overhauling the nation’s capital gains tax laws is aimed at fixing a broken housing market.

item.title: China Market Watchdog Sets Priorities to Support Private Sector
item.description: China’s market regulator set out 34 priorities for 2026 to support private sector growth, focusing on fair competition, stronger legal protections and efficient regulation.

item.title: Malaysia, Norway Tensions Deepen Over Scrapped Missile Deal
item.description: Tensions between Malaysia and Norway heightened over a scrapped weapons deal as Oslo confirmed it had revoked export licenses tied to a naval strike missile system.

item.title: Philippines Asks Court to Deny Petition of Senator Sought by ICC
item.description: The Philippine government asked the Supreme Court to deny a petition by Senator Ronald Dela Rosa to stop his arrest after the International Criminal Court issued a warrant on allegations of crimes against humanity.

item.title: Malaysia Unveils Measures to Help Airlines Hit by Iran War
item.description: Malaysia introduced measures to ensure the operational stability of its aviation industry and ease the financial strain on airlines as they struggle with the impact of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Vietnam-Bound Supertanker Halted by US Navy Resumes Journey
item.description: A Vietnam-bound supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude, which idled for days in the Gulf of Oman after it was halted by US forces, has resumed its journey.

item.title: Xi, Putin Hail Opening of China-Russia Expo, Stress Cooperation
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin exchanged congratulatory messages marking the opening of the China-Russia Expo in the northeasten city of Harbin, highlighting deepening economic ties between the two countries.

item.title: WHO Declares Congo Ebola Outbreak Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared an Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, warning that a rare virus strain with no approved vaccine or treatment may already be spreading more widely than detected.

item.title: India Missed Out on AI and Now Its Run as Market Darling May Be Over
item.description: India stands out as one of the biggest losers as the artificial intelligence trade reshapes global investment flows.

item.title: Global Inventory Race Intensifies in Shadow of the Iran War
item.description: The global rush to stockpile manufactured goods on fears of an energy-supply crunch will again overshadow business surveys in the coming week gauging the impact of a third month of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Public BDCs Are Pricing In Most Pain Since Covid
item.description: Private credit’s aggressive push to court retail investors has exposed the industry to a less familiar threat: public market volatility, which is dragging business development company shares toward their steepest discounts relative to their assets since the pandemic.

item.title: ASML Partners With Tata Electronics to Advance India Chip Plans
item.description: ASML Holding NV entered into a partnership agreement with Tata Electronics Private Limited aimed at ramping up India’s goal to develop domestic chip-manufacturing capabilities.

item.title: AI Poised to Tilt Job Market Leverage Toward Older Workers
item.description: When it comes to job cuts, older workers are often disproportionately affected. But a new survey of chief executive officers suggests this won’t be a given as companies adopt artificial intelligence.

item.title: India Tightens Silver Import Rules Amid Measures to Defend Rupee
item.description: India tightened rules for imports of silver, one of a number of measures to preserve foreign-exchange reserves and defend the rupee after the currency sank to an all-time low.

item.title: Harvard’s Endowment Chief Begins Plans to Depart from Fund
item.description: Harvard University’s endowment head, N.P. “Narv” Narvekar, has begun discussions about departing from the investment fund valued at $56.9 billion.

item.title: Latvia’s President Nominates Kulbergs to Form New Government
item.description: Latvia’s President Edgars Rinkevics nominated Andris Kulbergs of the United List party to form a new government after Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned on Thursday ahead of parliamentary elections in October.

item.title: Bond Selloff Threatens to Knock AI Stock Frenzy Off Course
item.description: Investors are fervently chasing the hot rally in tech and AI stocks while widely acknowledging that rising yields threaten to knock equities off course.

item.title: Retail Euphoria Turns Six-Week Fund Into Record-Busting AI Trade
item.description: Brian Emes manages a retail store in Lethbridge, Alberta, a small city on the Canadian prairie about a two-hour drive from the US border. On the morning of May 11, before opening the shop, the 43-year-old launched his brokerage app and bought 55 shares of an exchange-traded fund that hadn’t existed until early April.

item.title: Ray Dalio Talks about China, and Whitney Tilson Weighs in on Mamdani’s Feud With Griffin
item.description: President Donald Trump took a trip to Beijing this week, and Mamdani’s feud with Griffin heated up

item.title: How China Is Pushing Back Against the West’s De-Risking Strategy
item.description: Much of the world relies on China for an increasing number of products. Beijing wants to keep it that way.

item.title: Modest ECB Rate Hike Would Limit Economic Pain, Stournaras Says
item.description: A small European Central Bank interest-rate increase could temper inflation without causing economic damage, Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras told Liberal.gr.
